auditScope: First-time audit (file previously absent). Scanned signup/login docs, password-reset/recovery docs, theme/branding docs, and all files for veloso.dev references, per Step 0 instructions for a first-time audit. Did not re-derive prior ADRs (domain-generic policy, MFA passkey-only scope) which were treated as authoritative per user-supplied context.
architectureNote: This repo is a Docker Compose stack (authentik + PostgreSQL + Redis, see docker-compose.yml) wrapping goauthentik/authentik, a third-party prebuilt identity-provider image. There is no bespoke frontend source (no Next.js/React/HTML) in this repo for login, signup, password reset, or theming — authentik ships its own compiled web UI (Django-templated 'flow executor'). Branding is done via authentik's own 'Brand' object (logo/favicon/custom CSS) through its admin UI/API, and optionally by mounting custom Django templates into the /templates volume already wired in docker-compose.yml (${SSO_BASE_PATH}/authentik/custom-templates:/templates). Because of this, the product-frontend-scaffolder's UI-scaffolding actions (writing signup/login page components, a theme-toggle component, etc.) do not apply in the literal sense — there is no git-tracked frontend source to scaffold them into. The underlying invariants (password reset, passkey login, theme control, signature) are tracked below as configuration/documentation tasks against authentik instead, and the project is NOT recorded as a blanket non-applicable exception — each invariant is assessed on its own merits.

parentUmbrellaFrontend.notes: No separate umbrella product frontend exists or is implied by this repo. authentik's own web UI (login/flow executor + admin console) is the entire user-facing surface this project controls. There is no multi-product portal to scaffold here; authentik's built-in 'My Applications' launch page already serves that role once apps are registered. Nothing to scaffold in this repo.

signupPage.notes: No self-service signup flow exists, and none is planned. Per docs/first-sso-configuration.md, users are admin-created (admin sets an initial password or triggers authentik's recovery-link flow so the user sets their own). This is the correct posture for an internal SSO gateway that admin-controls access to NAS/self-hosted apps — open self-service signup would be a security regression, not a missing feature. Not tracked as a gap.

loginPage.notes: authentik's built-in login flow provides username/password. Passkey (WebAuthn) as a second factor is documented as active, in-scope work per ADR-012/docs/security-hardening.md/docs/first-sso-configuration.md §3, but TODO.md marks 'MFA recommendation applied' as unconfirmed/not yet done on the live instance (checkbox unchecked as of the last edit this session found) — per project memory, checkboxes are not to be trusted without live verification, and this sandbox cannot reach the live authentik instance to confirm. Email one-time-code as a forgotten-password fallback is NOT present: authentik has an email-based stage capability but nothing in this repo's docs configures it as a login-time OTP fallback. Rate-limiting of login attempts/code requests is not documented as configured (authentik supports it via flow policies/brute-force stage, but no doc references binding it).

passwordReset.notes: This is the user's explicit near-term ask (not yet implemented — audit only). authentik has a built-in 'Recovery' flow (tokenized, single-use, expiring email link) capable of satisfying this invariant, but docs/first-sso-configuration.md only references 'send recovery link' in the context of admin-initiated first-time account setup, not as a visible 'reset password' option surfaced on the login page itself. No doc or config currently wires a recovery flow into the default login/authentication flow's UI. This is a configuration task inside authentik's admin console (Flows & Stages) plus a doc update, not a code change — see report for details.

themeControl.notes: authentik ships its own built-in theme setting (a UI theme selector, historically light/dark/auto, exposed via the Brand object's default and per-user account settings) but this is NOT the mandated single cycling light→dark→system button — it's authentik's own native control, out of this repo's code. Building a literal cycling single-button control would require overriding authentik's compiled frontend assets or its Django flow templates, a heavy customization beyond typical custom-CSS/logo branding. Recorded as a known, currently-unaddressed deviation from the standard invariant; not silently dropped. Revisit if/when custom-templates work is undertaken for the branding request.

velosoSignature.notes: EXPLICIT CONFLICT, not silently resolved: the standard convention requires a veloso.dev binary-dot signature on every frontend page/app, including client/contract work. This repo has an established, deliberate policy (per prior work, confirmed by grep) to stay domain-generic — no veloso.dev references anywhere except the README 'Developer / Contact' line — so the repo stays reusable by other operators. The live deployment's actual brand is 'Systems Not Silos' (sso.systemsnotsilos.com), distinct from veloso.dev. Additionally, even if the signature were desired, authentik's login page is not this repo's frontend source — it would have to be injected via authentik's custom-templates mount, which is itself branding surface currently reserved for the Systems Not Silos rebrand the user is planning. No signature has been added anywhere. This needs an explicit decision from the user (keep repo generic and skip the veloso.dev signature entirely for this project, or find another way to satisfy the convention) before any implementation — do not decide silently in either direction.
